cyclotron at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory
Reaction Studies (STARS) was used to measure outgoing
deuterons and tritons over an angular range of 34deg
to 64deg. For this experiment STARS consisted of one
140 mu-m dE and two 1000 mu-m E1 and E2 silicon
detectors of Micron S2 type. Coincident fission
fragments were measured at backward angles between
109deg and 137deg using a second 140 mu-m Micron S2
type detector. Each S2 detector was segmented into
48 rings on one side and 16 sectors on the other.
For the present experiment the rings and sectors were
bussed together so each detector had 8 sectors and 24
one-millimeter rings to measure incident charged
The STARS chamber was surrounded by six clover
g-ray detectors from the Livermore-Berkeley Array
E-dE method was used to identify deuterons
Coincidence between STARS silicon array and
LIBERACE gamma detector array was required to measure
gamma rays in coincidence with fission fragments and
emitted charged particles. Single particles events,
